story: Dr. Patel has believed for years that a particular supplement reduces inflammation. When a new meta-analysis finds no significant effect, she dismisses it as poorly designed. She continues citing the three older studies that supported her view and ignores the dozen that did not. Her residents notice she only assigns readings that confirm the supplement's benefits.

beliefs: her judgment is more reliable than the meta-analysis
she is certain the supplement works
claims: older confirming studies are more trustworthy
